title: Angular Fundamentals
description: Components, dependency injection, lifecycle, directives, pipes and routing.

questions.question: What is a component in Angular, and what are the roles of the three main parts of its definition: the template, the class, and the metadata in the `@Component` decorator?
Explain the difference between property binding `[value]="x"`, event binding `(click)="f()"`, and two-way binding `[(ngModel)]="x"`. What is two-way binding actually syntactic sugar for?

How do parent and child components communicate in Angular? Compare `input()`/`output()` (or the decorator equivalents `@Input`/`@Output`) and explain when you would reach for a shared service instead.
How does Angular's dependency injection work? Explain what an injector is, what `providedIn: 'root'` means, and what changes when you instead list a service in a component's `providers` array.
Compare constructor injection with the `inject()` function. Why has `inject()` become the recommended style, and in which contexts can `inject()` be called at all (what is an 'injection context')?

Walk through the component lifecycle: what are `ngOnInit`, `ngOnChanges`, `ngAfterViewInit` and `ngOnDestroy` for, and why is initialization logic usually put in `ngOnInit` rather than the constructor?
What is the difference between a structural directive and an attribute directive? Give an example of each and explain what the `*` prefix (e.g. in `*ngIf`) tells the compiler to do.

Explain how the Angular Router matches a URL to a component. Cover route configuration, route parameters (`/users/:id`), and lazy loading a route with `loadComponent`/`loadChildren` — why does lazy loading matter for startup time?
